heading: Rate orders of State agencies
label: 1342 Rate orders of State agencies
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 41(1) (, par. 1, ; , ; , ; , ).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 2436 Stat. 1091May 14, 1934, ch. 283, § 148 Stat. 775Aug. 21, 1937, ch. 726, § 150 Stat. 738Apr. 20, 1940, ch. 11754 Stat. 143This section rearranges and restates the fourth sentence of , U.S.C., 1940 ed.section 41(1) of title 28Other provisions of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., are incorporated in sections 1331, 1332, 1341, 1345, 1354, and 1359 of this title.section 41(1) of title 28Words “at law or in equity” before “in the courts of such State” were omitted as unnecessary.Words “civil action” were substituted for “suit,” in view of Rule 2 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.Word “operation” was substituted for “enforcement, operation or execution” for the same reason.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 41(1) (, par. 1, ; , ; , ; , ).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 2436 Stat. 1091May 14, 1934, ch. 283, § 148 Stat. 775Aug. 21, 1937, ch. 726, § 150 Stat. 738Apr. 20, 1940, ch. 11754 Stat. 143This section rearranges and restates the fourth sentence of , U.S.C., 1940 ed.section 41(1) of title 28Other provisions of , U.S.C., 1940 ed., are incorporated in sections 1331, 1332, 1341, 1345, 1354, and 1359 of this title.section 41(1) of title 28Words “at law or in equity” before “in the courts of such State” were omitted as unnecessary.Words “civil action” were substituted for “suit,” in view of Rule 2 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.Word “operation” was substituted for “enforcement, operation or execution” for the same reason.

children.text: Jurisdiction is based solely on diversity of citizenship or repugnance of the order to the Federal Constitution; and,
The order does not interfere with interstate commerce; and,
The order has been made after reasonable notice and hearing; and,
A plain, speedy and efficient remedy may be had in the courts of such State.
